candidates.description: Add plistlib-based XML parsing with proper exception handling
Add comprehensive XML validation with schema checking and detailed error reporting

patches.rationale: FIX-001a provides the optimal balance: uses Python's built-in plistlib for proper XML parsing, adds exception handling, maintains backward compatibility, and is minimal enough to avoid introducing new bugs. FIX-001b is over-engineered for this use case.

monitoring: Check for new privacy compliance failures
Track Info.plist parsing errors in logs
rollback_triggers: Valid Info.plist files incorrectly flagged as malformed
Performance regression in privacy audit (>2x slower)
New exceptions not caught by exception handling

candidates.description: Add UTF-8 encoding parameter and per-file exception handling to all Swift scanning methods
Create helper method for safe file reading with encoding detection

patches.rationale: FIX-002a is the most direct fix: adds encoding='utf-8' to all read_text() calls and wraps each file read in try-except to identify problematic files. FIX-002b adds unnecessary abstraction for a simple fix.
patches.patch_diff:
```python
# File: orchestrator/pre_submission_testing.py
# Apply to lines: 365, 414, 529, 571, 613, 655

# Pattern to apply to ALL Swift file reading in these methods:
# _crash_test(), _completeness_check(), _hig_compliance_check(), 
# _offline_test(), _ipv6_test(), _code_execution_scan()

# BEFORE (line 365 in _crash_test):
#   content = view_file.read_text()

# AFTER (FIX-002a):
bad_files = []
for view_file in view_files[:10]:
    try:
        content = view_file.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
    except (UnicodeDecodeError, Exception) as e:
        bad_files.append(f"{view_file.name}: {str(e)}")
        continue  # Skip this file, continue with others
    
    # ... rest of processing logic

# At the end of method, report bad files:
if bad_files:
    crashes_found.extend([f"Unreadable file: {bf}" for bf in bad_files])

# Apply same pattern to all 6 methods:
# - Line 414 in _completeness_check()
# - Line 529 in _hig_compliance_check()
# - Line 571 in _offline_test()
# - Line 613 in _ipv6_test()
# - Line 655 in _code_execution_scan()
```

monitoring: Track number of files skipped due to encoding issues
rollback_triggers: Valid UTF-8 Swift files incorrectly skipped
New exceptions not caught by exception handling

candidates.description: Add input validation with MIN_NAME_LENGTH check and whitespace stripping
Add comprehensive ProjectSpec validation with fallback name generation

patches.rationale: FIX-003a adds minimal validation: strips whitespace, checks minimum length, provides fallback. This is sufficient for the use case. FIX-003b adds complexity that's not needed.
patches.patch_diff:
```python
# File: orchestrator/app_store_assets.py
# Lines: 140-170

# Add constant at class level:
MIN_NAME_LENGTH = 2  # App Store requires at least 2 characters

# Modify _generate_app_name method:
async def _generate_app_name(self, project: ProjectSpec) -> str:
    # FIX-003a: Validate project name before generating app name
    raw_name = getattr(project, 'name', '').strip()
    
    # Handle empty or whitespace-only names
    if not raw_name or len(raw_name) < self.MIN_NAME_LENGTH:
        # Generate fallback name from description or use generic
        if hasattr(project, 'description') and project.description:
            # Extract first few meaningful words from description
            words = project.description.split()[:3]
            raw_name = ' '.join(words) if words else 'MyApp'
        else:
            raw_name = 'MyApp'
    
    # Truncate if too long
    if len(raw_name) > self.MAX_NAME_LENGTH:
        raw_name = raw_name[:self.MAX_NAME_LENGTH].rsplit(' ', 1)[0]
    
    name = raw_name
    logger.debug(f"Generated app name: {name}")
    return name

# Apply same validation to _generate_subtitle:
async def _generate_subtitle(self, project: ProjectSpec) -> str:
    # Similar validation for subtitle
    raw_subtitle = getattr(project, 'description', '').strip()
    
    if not raw_subtitle or len(raw_subtitle) < 5:
        raw_subtitle = f"Professional {self._extract_category(project)} app"
    
    # Truncate if needed
    if len(raw_subtitle) > self.MAX_SUBTITLE_LENGTH:
        raw_subtitle = raw_subtitle[:self.MAX_SUBTITLE_LENGTH].rsplit(' ', 1)[0]
    
    logger.debug(f"Generated subtitle: {raw_subtitle}")
    return raw_subtitle
```

monitoring: Monitor app name generation success rate
Check for fallback name usage in logs
Track App Store metadata validation errors
rollback_triggers: Valid project names incorrectly replaced with fallback
Generated names violate App Store guidelines
User complaints about unexpected name changes
